question.text: A speculator expects natural gas prices to fall. He sells natural gas futures at Rs 450 per unit (lot: 1,250 units). If the price drops to Rs 420 at expiry, the profit is:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Short payoff per unit = F - ST = 450 - 420 = Rs 30. Total = 30 × 1,250 = Rs 37,500. **The Trap:** Rs 30 is per unit. Rs 3,750 uses 125 units. Rs 375 uses 12.5 units. Always multiply per-unit P&L by the full lot size. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I covers similar payoff calculations with lot size. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Natural gas is among the most volatile commodities — speculative positions should be sized conservatively relative to capital, even when the view is strongly directional.
